title: thalictrum minus proles corymbulosum
intro: Thalictrum minus proles corymbulosum, also known as meadow rue, is a flowering plant in the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae) that is native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing and reducing inflammation. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and relieving digestive issues, and it has been used in herbal preparations like teas and infusions to take advantage of its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often harvested in the summer months and dried for later use in herbal remedies.
